_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse and verify the sources of data and information we use in our economic investigations.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse and verify the sources of data and information we use in our economic investigations.
y: Verifying sources protects us from building conclusions on unreliable or biased data.
Source analysis is critical in economics where data can be manipulated to support particular agendas.
These skills help us become discerning consumers of economic information in our daily lives.
eg: Checking who produced a set of economic data and whether they have any potential bias.
Comparing data from a government source with data from a private organisation on the same topic.
Analysing whether a source's methodology is sound and its conclusions are justified.
Identifying potential conflicts of interest in sources that present economic arguments.
Cross-referencing a claim made in a news article with the original data source.
Developing criteria for evaluating the trustworthiness of economic data sources.
